pageMeta.title: AI Agents for Building Construction Teams
pageMeta.description: DocsBot helps building construction teams answer project, safety, and client questions faster with source-grounded AI agents and workflow handoffs.

hero.eyebrow: AI automation for contractors, project teams, and client support
hero.headline: Keep construction knowledge moving from office to field
hero.subheadline: DocsBot helps building construction companies create AI agents trained on project documents, SOPs, safety guidance, service pages, FAQs, and internal knowledge so teams can get source-grounded answers, summarize context, collect request details, and route the next step without digging through folders or message threads.

proofBullets.text: Answer project, service, warranty, and documentation questions from approved construction knowledge sources.
Help field and office teams find SOPs, safety procedures, submittal notes, specs, and onboarding materials faster.
Collect estimate or project inquiry details and hand off qualified requests to sales, operations, or support workflows.
Use configured AI Actions to look up status, create tasks or tickets, summarize issues, and escalate sensitive work to people.

businessClarity.whatDocsBotDoes: DocsBot turns a construction company’s approved knowledge into AI support and internal knowledge agents. Those agents can answer from project FAQs, service catalogs, SOPs, safety manuals, warranty policies, onboarding docs, PDFs, websites, and internal work instructions; then, when configured, use Skills, APIs, connectors, or workflow automations to route requests, create records, summarize context, or prepare handoffs.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Keep answers consistent when schedules, RFIs, specs, meeting notes, and client questions are spread across shared drives, email, and project systems.

bullets.text: Retrieve approved procedures, closeout requirements, submittal guidance, and meeting context without interrupting the whole project team.
Summarize recurring questions and identify documentation gaps that cause avoidable back-and-forth.
Route unclear, contractual, or safety-sensitive questions to the right human reviewer with conversation context attached.

whoItHelps.roleContext: Support crews with quick access to approved jobsite procedures, safety references, equipment instructions, and issue-escalation paths.

bullets.text: Make safety talks, PPE reminders, incident steps, and SOPs searchable from a private team chat experience.
OSHA identifies falls, struck-by incidents, caught-in or between hazards, and electrocutions as major construction Focus Four hazards.
Collect structured incident or quality issue details and prepare a handoff for review instead of relying on fragmented messages.

whoItHelps.role: Owners, estimators, and business development teams
whoItHelps.roleContext: Help prospects and clients get fast answers about services, project fit, documents needed, timelines, and next steps while keeping humans in control of pricing, contracts, and commitments.

bullets.text: Answer service, capability, location, warranty, and process questions from approved website and sales materials.
Collect lead details such as project type, location, timeline, scope, budget range, and preferred follow-up channel.
Forward qualified inquiries, summaries, and scheduling intent through customer-configured CRM, email, form, or calendar workflows.

evidenceSources.claim: The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics maintains current employment, openings, turnover, wage, and projection data for NAICS 23 construction.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: Construction: NAICS 23 — Industries at a Glance

evidenceSources.metric: 94% of surveyed firms with openings struggled to fill positions
evidenceSources.claim: AGC reported that among surveyed construction firms with openings, 94% had trouble filling at least some positions, especially craft roles.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: New Survey Shows How Nation’s Failure To Invest In Construction Education & Training Programs Makes It Hard For Firms To Build

evidenceSources.claim: OSHA construction training materials identify falls, caught-in or between, struck-by, and electrocution hazards as the construction Focus Four.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: OSHA Training Institute Construction Focus Four: Caught-In or -Between Hazards

evidenceSources.metric: 75% of struck-by fatalities involve heavy equipment
evidenceSources.claim: OSHA states that approximately 75% of struck-by fatalities involve heavy equipment such as trucks or cranes.

evidenceSources.metric: Focus Four hazards cause nearly 60% of construction fatalities
evidenceSources.claim: NIOSH notes that OSHA’s Focus Four hazards represent the causes of nearly 60% of construction fatalities each year.
evidenceSources.sourceTitle: Preventing Electrocution of Construction Contract Workers

agentUseCases.name: Project document and RFI knowledge assistant
agentUseCases.problem: Construction teams lose time when project procedures, specs, RFIs, submittals, and owner requirements are buried in PDFs, folders, wikis, and message threads.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: Train a private DocsBot agent on approved project documentation, SOPs, templates, owner-facing FAQs, closeout requirements, and internal notes. Team members ask natural-language questions and receive source-grounded answers with citations back to approved materials. Unclear or contractual questions can be escalated with a summary for a project manager.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: With configured Skills, APIs, connectors, or MCP-connected tools, the agent can look up approved project status, create a task, or attach a summarized question to a review workflow.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: What closeout documents are required for this project type?
Where is our standard RFI response procedure?
Summarize the open documentation issues from this conversation for the project manager.
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Reduce repetitive searching and make project knowledge easier to reuse while keeping final contractual decisions with the responsible team.

agentUseCases.name: Safety and field procedure assistant
agentUseCases.problem: Field teams need quick access to current procedures, but safety-critical answers must stay grounded in approved company materials and escalate when judgment is required.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot can answer from safety manuals, toolbox talk libraries, incident response steps, equipment procedures, and jobsite SOPs. The agent can provide source-grounded references, collect missing context, and route urgent or safety-sensitive issues to a supervisor instead of giving unsupported advice.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: Customer-configured AI Actions can collect incident details, create a safety review task, notify the right team, or generate a structured handoff for a safety lead.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Show the approved ladder inspection checklist for today’s crew.
What is our process for reporting a near miss?
Create a summary for the safety manager with location, crew, equipment, and issue details.
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Give crews faster access to approved procedures while preserving human review for high-impact jobsite decisions.

agentUseCases.name: Client and homeowner project support agent
agentUseCases.problem: Clients often ask the same questions about timelines, site access, change requests, warranties, documentation, and what happens next.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: Deploy a DocsBot support agent on the website or client portal trained on company FAQs, service pages, project handbooks, warranty policies, and onboarding documents. The agent answers common questions, asks clarifying questions, and escalates issues that require a person.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: When configured, the agent can collect request details, create a ticket, route warranty questions, forward a summary to the project team, or trigger a follow-up workflow.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: What information do you need before estimating my renovation?
How do I request a change to the project scope?
Can you send this warranty question to the right team with my address and photos?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Improve response speed for routine client questions while keeping pricing, legal, and contract commitments under human control.

agentUseCases.name: Estimator and presales qualification agent
agentUseCases.problem: Sales and estimating teams need complete project information before they can evaluate fit, but web forms and calls often arrive with missing scope, location, and timeline details.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot can answer service-fit questions from approved sales materials, collect structured lead details, qualify inquiries using customer-defined rules, and prepare a concise handoff for estimators or sales. The agent can also support booking intent through approved scheduling workflows.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: AI Actions can submit a custom lead form, forward qualified inquiries to CRM or email workflows, request a meeting, and notify the right regional or service-line team.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Do you handle commercial tenant improvements in my area?
What documents should I prepare before requesting an estimate?
Collect my scope, timeline, budget range, and preferred follow-up time.
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Help estimating teams prioritize better-qualified opportunities and reduce back-and-forth before the first consultation.

agentUseCases.name: Training and onboarding knowledge agent
agentUseCases.problem: Contractors facing hiring pressure need consistent ways to help new employees learn procedures, tools, safety expectations, and company standards.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: Train DocsBot on onboarding guides, HR policies, role-specific SOPs, tool instructions, benefits materials, and training documents. Employees can ask questions privately, get consistent answers, and surface gaps where training content is missing or unclear.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: Analytics, logs, topic reports, and conversation review can show repeated employee questions and knowledge gaps for operations or HR to improve.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Where do I find the new-hire equipment checklist?
What is the process for submitting time from a jobsite?
Which SOP explains photo documentation standards?
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Make institutional knowledge more accessible as teams hire, reassign, and train workers in a tight labor market.

agentUseCases.name: Quality issue and punch-list intake assistant
agentUseCases.problem: Quality issues and punch-list items become harder to resolve when photos, locations, responsible trades, specs, and notes are scattered across emails and field messages.
agentUseCases.agentWorkflow: DocsBot can guide users through a structured intake flow, summarize the issue, point to relevant approved QA procedures, and route the item to a supervisor or operations team for review. It can answer from quality manuals, inspection checklists, finish standards, and documentation templates.
agentUseCases.liveContextOrTools: With approved Skills or APIs, the agent can create a task or ticket, update approved fields, attach a summary, and notify the relevant team or subcontractor coordinator.
exampleQuestionsOrRequests: Log this drywall finish issue with room number, photo notes, and trade involved.
What is our QA checklist before requesting final inspection?
Summarize this punch-list conversation for the superintendent.
agentUseCases.businessOutcome: Standardize issue intake and reduce missing context before quality problems are reviewed or assigned.

customerFacingAgent.headline: Give clients and prospects a source-grounded construction support experience
customerFacingAgent.intro: A customer-facing DocsBot agent can sit on your website, portal, or support flow to answer from approved construction content, collect project details, qualify inquiries, and hand off sensitive or complex requests to your team.

requests.request: “Can you build my project, and what do you need before an estimate?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Answer from service pages and estimator FAQs, then collect project type, address or region, scope, timeline, budget range, drawings, and preferred contact method.
requests.optionalAction: Submit a lead form or forward a qualified summary to the sales or estimating workflow configured by the customer.

requests.request: “What is the status of my warranty or service request?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Explain the documented process, collect identifying details, and, if live access is configured, look up approved status context or route the request to support.
requests.optionalAction: Create or update a support ticket, attach a summary, and escalate when a person needs to review the claim.

requests.request: “How do change requests work once construction has started?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Provide the approved change-request process and boundaries, explain what information is needed, and avoid making pricing or contract commitments automatically.
requests.optionalAction: Collect scope details and route the request to the project manager for review.

requests.request: “Can you send me the right closeout, care, or maintenance document?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Search approved closeout packets, maintenance instructions, product manuals, and FAQs to provide the most relevant source-grounded answer.
requests.optionalAction: Prepare an email or ticket summary with the requested document reference for staff review or follow-up.

requests.request: “Do you support clients in my language?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Use DocsBot multilingual answering capabilities to help customers ask questions in more than 100 languages when the company has provided approved source content.
requests.optionalAction: Escalate the conversation with the full transcript when a human needs to continue the response.

requests.request: “Who should I contact about site access, parking, or delivery rules?”
requests.agentResponseStrategy: Answer from project communication guides and route the customer to the correct team when a site-specific decision is needed.
requests.optionalAction: Notify the assigned coordinator or create a task with the customer’s details and question.

internalAgent.headline: Put construction SOPs, project knowledge, and team answers in one private AI agent
internalAgent.intro: DocsBot can serve as a private internal knowledge agent for office staff, field leadership, HR, safety, sales, and operations. Teams ask questions in a private chat experience and receive answers grounded in company-controlled sources.

workflows.name: Field SOP lookup
workflows.problem: Supervisors need quick answers without calling the office for every procedure, checklist, or form.
workflows.agentWorkflow: Train the agent on SOPs, jobsite forms, toolbox talks, inspection checklists, equipment procedures, and escalation instructions. The agent answers with grounded references and recommends escalation for ambiguous or high-risk scenarios.
workflows.optionalAction: Create a task, notify a safety lead, or prepare a structured handoff when configured.
workflows.outcome: Faster access to approved field knowledge without replacing supervisor judgment.

workflows.name: Operations and admin policy support
workflows.problem: Office teams repeatedly answer questions about payroll, time entry, purchasing, document control, travel, expense, and onboarding procedures.
workflows.agentWorkflow: DocsBot can train on internal HR, finance, operations, procurement, and onboarding documents so employees get consistent answers around administrative processes.
workflows.optionalAction: Route unresolved questions to HR, finance, or operations with a conversation summary.
workflows.outcome: Reduce repetitive internal questions and help employees follow the right process the first time.

workflows.name: Sales enablement for estimators and business development
workflows.problem: Estimators and sales teams need accurate service, capability, proposal, and objection-handling information while preparing responses.
workflows.agentWorkflow: Train DocsBot on service catalogs, proposal templates, case studies, qualification rules, pricing guidance, and implementation notes. The agent can draft responses and summarize prospect context for human review.
workflows.optionalAction: Draft follow-up emails, prepare account notes, or create a CRM task through configured workflows.
workflows.outcome: Give sales and estimating teams faster access to approved language while keeping final quotes and commitments human-owned.

workflows.name: Knowledge gap reporting
workflows.problem: Repeated unanswered questions can reveal missing procedures, outdated documentation, or unclear client-facing information.
workflows.agentWorkflow: Use DocsBot analytics, logs, topic management, reports, conversation review, and satisfaction or resolution analysis to spot recurring topics and improve the source materials behind the agent.
workflows.optionalAction: Turn repeated unanswered topics into documentation tasks or review workflows when configured.
workflows.outcome: Improve the documentation that drives both customer support and internal operations.

docsbotFeatureMapping.feature: Source-grounded AI agents trained on your content
docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot creates AI agents trained on company-controlled sources such as websites, help centers, PDFs, documents, internal content, and other configured knowledge bases.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Train agents on construction SOPs, project guides, safety manuals, service pages, warranty policies, estimator FAQs, and client handbooks so answers reflect your approved materials.

docsbotFeatureMapping.feature: Website widget and private team chat
docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot agents can work in customer-facing chat widgets and private team chat experiences, supporting both external support and internal knowledge workflows.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Put a support agent on your website for prospects and clients, and a private knowledge agent in the tools your project, safety, and operations teams already use.

docsbotFeatureMapping.feature: AI Actions, Skills, APIs, connectors, and MCP options
docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot AI Actions and Skills let agents take bounded, customer-configured actions such as lookups, task creation, routing, summaries, record updates, workflow triggers, and handoffs.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Use approved live context to check request status, create a quality task, route a warranty issue, submit lead details, or notify the right project stakeholder without giving the agent unlimited access.

docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot can connect to helpdesk-style workflows through customer-configured Skills, APIs, connectors, exported sources, or approved workflow access to summarize, route, answer, or draft ticket responses.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Support warranty, service, client, subcontractor, and internal operations tickets with grounded responses and summaries while escalating complex cases to people.

docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot provides analytics, logs, topic management, reports, conversation review, and knowledge-gap discovery so teams can see what users ask and where content is missing.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Find repeated construction questions about closeout, warranties, safety procedures, change requests, or onboarding and improve the documentation behind those answers.

docsbotFeatureMapping.feature: Security and governance for private knowledge
docsbotFeatureMapping.mainLandingPageContext: DocsBot offers SOC 2 Type II certification, GDPR-ready governance, encryption in transit and at rest, granular access controls, role-based permissions, and does not use customer data to train public models.
docsbotFeatureMapping.industryApplication: Use permissioned sources and scoped actions for project, employee, client, and operational content that should not be exposed broadly across the company or public web.

trustClaims: Train agents on your own construction content library, not generic answers.
Support high-volume question handling with source-grounded AI assistance.
Use analytics to see what clients, employees, and stakeholders keep asking.
trustAndProof.whyTheseReviewsFit: Construction teams need trusted answers from their own documents, fast first-line support, and visibility into recurring questions. DocsBot’s proof from support, documentation, and knowledge-base use cases shows how teams can make approved information easier to access across customers and staff.

faq.question: Can DocsBot answer project questions without guessing from outdated specs or random internet information?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot agents are trained on the sources you approve, such as project guides, SOPs, closeout requirements, warranty policies, service pages, PDFs, and internal work instructions. Answers can be source-grounded so project teams can verify where the response came from, and unclear, contractual, or high-risk questions can be routed to a human reviewer.

faq.question: How would this fit into the way our project managers already handle RFIs, submittals, closeout, and client questions?
faq.answer: DocsBot is best used as a knowledge and handoff layer, not as a replacement for your project management system. Teams can ask natural-language questions across approved procedures, project FAQs, templates, meeting notes, and closeout docs. With configured Skills, APIs, connectors, or workflows, the agent can prepare summaries, create tasks, or attach context to a review process.

faq.question: Can we use an AI agent for safety procedures without creating liability if the answer is wrong?
faq.answer: DocsBot should be configured to answer only from approved safety manuals, toolbox talks, incident steps, equipment procedures, and jobsite SOPs, with escalation rules for urgent or judgment-heavy situations. That matters because OSHA training materials identify falls, caught-in or between, struck-by, and electrocution as construction Focus Four hazards.

faq.question: Will field supervisors actually use this if they are on a jobsite and not sitting at a desk?
faq.answer: Adoption usually depends on making the agent useful for fast lookups: ladder checklists, near-miss reporting steps, PPE reminders, photo documentation standards, equipment procedures, and escalation contacts. A private team chat experience can give supervisors a familiar way to search approved procedures without calling the office for every form or checklist.

faq.question: Can DocsBot collect jobsite incident details or punch-list context before a person reviews it?
faq.answer: Yes. With customer-configured AI Actions, DocsBot can guide a user through structured intake, collect fields such as location, crew, equipment, trade involved, photos, and issue description, then create a task or ticket, notify the right team, or prepare a summary for a safety lead, superintendent, or operations reviewer.

faq.question: How do we keep pricing, contract language, change orders, and warranty decisions under human control?
faq.answer: You can set the agent’s role around intake, explanation, summarization, and routing. For pricing, scope changes, contract commitments, legal language, or disputed warranty claims, DocsBot can collect details and hand off the conversation with context instead of making the decision automatically.
faq.role: Owners, estimators, and business development teams

faq.question: Can a customer-facing agent qualify estimate requests before they reach our estimators?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can answer from approved service pages and estimator FAQs, then collect structured details such as project type, location, scope, timeline, budget range, drawings, and preferred follow-up channel. With configured workflows, it can forward qualified summaries to CRM, email, forms, scheduling, or other approved sales processes.
faq.role: Owners, estimators, and business development teams

faq.question: What ROI should we look for if we already have project management software?
faq.answer: Look for time saved on repetitive document searches, fewer incomplete intake requests, faster routing of client or warranty questions, better onboarding consistency, and less back-and-forth around SOPs and closeout requirements. The construction labor market makes these gains important: AGC reported that 94% of surveyed firms with openings struggled to fill at least some positions.

faq.question: Can DocsBot connect with our existing construction systems, ticketing tools, or CRM?
faq.answer: DocsBot can use customer-configured Skills, APIs, connectors, workflow automations, and MCP-connected tools to support approved lookups and actions. That can include checking request status, creating a task, forwarding a qualified lead, updating approved fields, or routing a warranty issue. Specific connections depend on what systems and permissions you configure.

faq.question: How do we avoid exposing project, employee, or client information to the wrong audience?
faq.answer: DocsBot supports governance controls for private knowledge, including SOC 2 Type II certification, GDPR-ready governance, encryption in transit and at rest, granular access controls, role-based permissions, and a policy that customer data is not used to train public models. Buyers should scope sources, permissions, and actions by audience before rollout.

faq.question: Can we separate public website answers from internal project or safety knowledge?
faq.answer: Yes. A construction company can maintain different agents or source scopes for different audiences. A public website agent might answer service, warranty, and estimate-prep questions, while a private internal agent can search SOPs, safety procedures, HR policies, project templates, and operations documents with role-based access controls.

faq.question: What does implementation look like if our documents are scattered across PDFs, folders, wikis, and websites?
faq.answer: A practical rollout starts by choosing one workflow, such as estimator intake, warranty support, field SOP lookup, or closeout questions. Then you identify approved sources, remove obsolete documents, set escalation rules, test real questions from your team, and review analytics to find gaps before expanding to more departments.

faq.question: How do we handle old versions of SOPs, specs, or safety documents?
faq.answer: Treat the agent as only as reliable as the source set you approve. Before launch, assign owners for safety manuals, SOPs, project templates, and client-facing policies. Retire duplicate or outdated files, use clear naming and versioning, and review unanswered or low-confidence conversations to identify content that needs cleanup.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help with onboarding new hires and cross-training office or field staff?
faq.answer: Yes. DocsBot can train on onboarding guides, HR policies, role-specific SOPs, tool instructions, benefits materials, safety expectations, and internal procedures. Employees can ask private questions and get consistent answers, while analytics reveal repeated questions or missing training content. This is useful in a market where AGC reported widespread hiring difficulty among firms with openings.

faq.question: Will the agent answer questions in Spanish or other languages for crews and clients?
faq.answer: DocsBot can answer in more than 100 languages when the company has provided approved source content. For construction teams, that can support multilingual client FAQs, service pages, safety references, onboarding content, and support workflows, while still escalating sensitive or ambiguous conversations to a person with the transcript attached.

faq.question: What analytics will operations leaders get after launch?
faq.answer: DocsBot provides analytics, logs, topic management, reports, conversation review, satisfaction or resolution analysis, and knowledge-gap discovery. For construction teams, that can reveal recurring questions about change requests, closeout packets, warranty steps, site logistics, onboarding, safety procedures, or missing project documentation.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help reduce repeated calls and messages to project coordinators?
faq.answer: Yes, if the repeated questions are documented somewhere reliable. DocsBot can answer routine questions from approved client handbooks, project FAQs, site access instructions, warranty policies, closeout documents, and internal SOPs. When the answer depends on a site-specific decision, it can collect context and route the request to the coordinator.

faq.question: How should we compare DocsBot with a generic chatbot on our website?
faq.answer: A generic chatbot may only collect leads or provide scripted answers. DocsBot is positioned as a source-grounded AI agent trained on your approved construction knowledge, with options for internal knowledge search, customer support, structured intake, analytics, human escalation, and bounded actions through customer-configured Skills, APIs, connectors, or workflows.

faq.question: Can the agent create tickets or tasks for warranty, quality, or service issues?
faq.answer: Yes, when you configure the appropriate workflow. DocsBot can collect issue details, summarize the conversation, and use approved Skills, APIs, connectors, or automations to create a task or ticket, update allowed fields, notify the right team, or route the request for human review.

faq.question: How do we keep subcontractor, owner, and internal employee questions from getting mixed together?
faq.answer: Use separate source scopes, instructions, and access rules for each audience. For example, subcontractors might see project communication rules and documentation procedures, clients might see warranty and site access FAQs, and employees might access SOPs, HR policies, or safety manuals. Role-based permissions help prevent oversharing.

faq.question: Can DocsBot support safety reminders without pretending to be a safety manager?
faq.answer: Yes. The agent can retrieve approved checklists, toolbox talks, reporting procedures, and escalation paths, while making it clear that supervisors or safety leads own final judgment. That boundary is important in construction, where NIOSH notes that OSHA’s Focus Four hazards account for nearly 60% of construction fatalities each year.

faq.question: What happens when someone asks a question that is not covered in our documents?
faq.answer: DocsBot can be configured to avoid unsupported answers, ask clarifying questions, cite available sources, or escalate to a person when the knowledge base is incomplete. Logs and knowledge-gap reporting then help your team see which missing topics should become new SOPs, FAQs, client guidance, or training content.

faq.question: Can estimators use DocsBot internally, not just on the website?
faq.answer: Yes. An internal sales enablement agent can be trained on service catalogs, proposal templates, qualification rules, pricing guidance, case studies, objection handling, and customer-facing FAQs. It can draft follow-up language or prepare account notes for human review, while leaving quotes, contract terms, and commitments to the estimator or owner.
faq.role: Owners, estimators, and business development teams

faq.question: How do we measure whether the agent is safe enough to expand from one department to the whole company?
faq.answer: Start with a controlled pilot and track answer quality, escalation rates, unresolved questions, source coverage, user feedback, and workflow completion. Review logs with the process owners for that department before adding more sources or actions. Expansion should follow evidence that the agent is answering from approved content and escalating appropriately.

faq.question: Can DocsBot help with heavy equipment or struck-by hazard procedures?
faq.answer: DocsBot can make approved equipment procedures, traffic control guidance, inspection checklists, and incident reporting steps easier to find, while escalating urgent jobsite decisions to supervisors. OSHA states that approximately 75% of struck-by fatalities involve heavy equipment such as trucks or cranes, so these workflows should be tightly governed.

faq.question: Who should own the agent after procurement: IT, operations, safety, or project management?
faq.answer: Most construction teams need shared ownership. IT should review security, access, and connections; operations or project leadership should own workflow design; safety should approve safety content and escalation rules; and sales or estimating should own customer-facing qualification logic. DocsBot analytics can then guide ongoing source improvements.
